question: What's the best time to visit Brunswick Heads NSW with kids?
answer: G'day! Spring (September-November) and autumn (March-May) are absolutely ripper for visiting Brunswick Heads with little ones. You'll dodge the summer crowds and enjoy mild weather perfect for beach days. The water's still lovely for swimming, and you won't be battling school holiday prices. That said, if you don't mind a bit of hustle and bustle, summer's magic here – just book well ahead!

question: Are there pet friendly holiday parks in Brunswick Heads NSW?
answer: You're in luck, mate! Several holiday parks around Brunswick Heads welcome your furry family members. Most pet-friendly parks require dogs to be leashed and have specific pet-designated cabins or sites. Always ring ahead to confirm their pet policy and any additional fees. Brunswick Heads itself is super dog-friendly with off-leash areas at the beach, so Fido will have a ball!

question: How much do holiday parks cost in Brunswick Heads NSW during school holidays?
answer: During school holidays, expect to pay around $150-$350 per night for a family cabin, depending on size and amenities. Powered sites for caravans run $50-$100 nightly. Easter and Christmas holidays are priciest. Top tip: book 6-12 months ahead for school holidays to snag better rates and availability. Off-peak visits can save you 30-50% – worth considering if you've got flexibility!

question: What should I pack for a holiday park stay with young kids?
answer: Don't stress! Pack swimmers, rashies, sun hats, and reef-safe sunscreen – that's your essentials sorted. Bring beach toys, bikes or scooters for park fun, and insect repellent for dusk. Most cabins have basic kitchen gear, but bring your own tea towels and favourite cooking bits. Chuck in some board games for rainy days, first aid kit, and your kids' favourite snacks for emergencies!

question: What is there to do near Brunswick Heads NSW holiday parks for families?
answer: Heaps to keep the kiddos entertained! Brunswick Heads has gorgeous calm river beaches perfect for littlies, plus rockpool exploring at Torakina Beach. Take a scenic river walk to Byron Bay, hire kayaks or SUPs, or try fishing off the jetty. Pop to nearby Byron for the lighthouse and beaches, or visit Crystal Castle gardens. Many holiday parks have pools, playgrounds and jumping pillows too!
